summary: Rename `apply`→`emit`: re-emit each projection whole and byte-deterministically, double-emit verified; delete the residual three-state merge model

new.description: The renamed apply suite driving `temper emit`: idempotence over a clean surface, a surface edit re-emits, --dry-run writes nothing, and a NEW double-emit-determinism assertion (emit twice → byte-identical projection + lock). Carries over tests/apply.rs's cases minus the retired Conflicted-as-world-drift semantics.

edit.description: Rename the `Apply` command variant to `Emit`, its CLI name to `emit`, and add the `--frozen` (refuse network — CI posture) + keep `--dry-run` flags per the CLI surface (20-surface CLI section); dispatch to `drift::emit`.

Rename `apply`→`emit`/`render_apply`→`render_emit`; make emit re-emit each projection whole from the authored source (project_bytes already does) and add a double-emit verification (emit twice, compare bytes — nondeterminism is a loud failure, never silent churn). Delete the residual desired/last-applied/real merge model + the `Conflicted`-as-world-drift outcome (a hand-edited projection is drift routed to the source, surfaced by config.stale/guard, not a mergeable conflict). Keep emit_hash freshness.

Update the `install::gate_installed`/placement callers that name `drift::apply`/`ApplyOutcome` to the renamed `emit` symbols (install consumes drift::place, unchanged; only the apply-named references move).
Rename any `apply` driving to `emit`.

tests.asserts: `temper emit` re-emits whole and is idempotent; double-emit yields byte-identical output; --dry-run writes nothing; no three-state conflict state remains.

acceptance: `temper emit [--frozen] [--dry-run]` compiles each projection whole and byte-deterministically, double-emit verified; the three-state merge model is deleted; a hand-edited projection is drift (not a Conflicted merge state); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: apply already re-emits whole; the deltas are the rename, --frozen, the double-emit determinism check, and deleting the desired/last-applied/real internals READD-RETIRE left standing. The two-projectors bug stays LIVE until EMIT-OWNED-PLACEMENTS — do not rely on bare emit over rules until it lands. tests/apply.rs→tests/emit.rs.

summary: Serialize the full manifest at import — bindings, requirements, member features, and edges into `temper.toml`, patch-preserving any hand-authored manifest

edit.description: Grow `AuthorLayer` into the full manifest model: beyond the optional overrides it parses today, model+serialize the generated-canonical manifest — kind→package bindings, the requirements roster, member features (per the closed extraction vocabulary), and declared/mention edges. This is the shape both import (write) and check (read, next entry) share.

At import, serialize that manifest to `temper.toml` — the generated-canonical artifact the gate will read. A hand-authored floor manifest is patched format-preserving (`toml_edit`), NEVER clobbered (20-surface: 'a hand-written manifest is patched format-preserving'). import still writes the `.temper/` document copies + lock here; INIT-ONRAMP drops the copy later.
Add coverage: import writes a temper.toml manifest carrying the bindings/requirements/features/edges; re-import patch-preserves a hand-authored manifest (no clobber, comments/order kept).

tests.asserts: import serializes the manifest (bindings, requirements, member features, edges) into temper.toml; a pre-existing hand-authored manifest is patched format-preserving, not overwritten.

acceptance: import writes the `temper.toml` manifest serializing every declaration the gate/read verbs consume; a hand-authored floor manifest survives format-preserving; check still reads the `.temper/` copy at this step; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Write-side only — the read flip is MANIFEST-GATE-READ. The manifest is generated-canonical (committed, gate-read, hand-writable at the floor). AuthorLayer becomes the manifest model, shared with the next entry (both edit compose.rs — serial). Keeps the copy tree for now so check stays green until the read flips.

summary: Make `check` read the `temper.toml` manifest as its gate corpus — manifest + lock + authored sources, not the `.temper/` copy tree; add the `config.stale` finding

per.section: The surface: the assembly over its contents

edit.description: Flip `Workspace::load`/`surface_units` to read the manifest as the corpus: document/module members arrive pre-extracted from the manifest (bounded by the closed vocabulary), the lock supplies freshness — stop ranging the `.temper/` copy tree as the declaration source. Conformance + admissibility unchanged in shape (two greens); the gate reads no language runtime.

Complete the manifest READER begun in MANIFEST-EMIT: load bindings/requirements/features/edges from `temper.toml` into the structures the roster/graph/coverage tier consumes (today only overrides are read).

Rewire `gate()` (main.rs:681) so the manifest is the primary declaration input (not an optional overlay): load it once, pass its features/edges/roster to conformance/admissibility/graph, and run the freshness check.

Add the `config.stale` finding: the committed manifest/projection does not match the lock's `source_hash`/`emit_hash` pair — one finding pointing at whichever side moved. Exposed to check via the gate.

Rework the layering tests: the manifest is the corpus now, not an overlay over a copy-tree floor. Assert check reads bindings/requirements/features from it and reports config.stale on a mismatched fingerprint.
Update the import→check flow: check now reads the manifest; assert a clean manifest checks green and a stale projection surfaces config.stale.

tests.asserts: check loads the temper.toml manifest as its declaration source (bindings, requirements, member features, edges) and reports config.stale when the committed manifest/projection mismatches the lock.

acceptance: `check` reads the manifest + lock + authored sources as its gate corpus (no `.temper/` copy ranging, no runtime), validates conformance/admissibility/freshness, and reports `config.stale`; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Biggest entry — the manifest becomes the gate's corpus (20-surface: 'the only thing the gate reads'). config.stale's projection half stands on E2's lock, its manifest half on the manifest here. document-carried members are still copied into `.temper/` (INIT-ONRAMP drops that); in-place live-extraction lands in INIT-ONRAMP when in-place members first appear.

summary: Rename `import`→`init`: scan an existing harness into a config skeleton over members in place — zero file moves, no `.temper/` copy tree

per.section: Decision: `init` is the on-ramp, and adoption is a gradient

edit.description: Rename the `Import` command to `Init` (CLI `init [<harness-path>]`), add a per-member `init --lift` that migrates one member up a carriage rung; dispatch to `import::run` renamed to the init on-ramp.

Drop the copy-to-`.temper/` step: init scans the harness and generates the manifest + skeleton over members IN PLACE (zero file moves, zero reformatting — a 40-artifact harness lands byte-identical, floor-governed, day one). In-place members carry no provenance (the file is its own source). `--lift` is the per-member migration that normalizes framing, never content.

Add the in-place read path: an in-place member's features are LIVE-extracted from its landscape file (`.claude/rules/x.md`), its joins declared in the manifest — no projection, no drift. Document/module members still arrive pre-extracted from the manifest (MANIFEST-GATE-READ).
emit skips in-place members (no projection to compile); only document/module-carried members emit. In-place members' only freshness fact is the repository's own — they cannot drift.
Rename the `import` helper/driving to `init`; assert init leaves members byte-identical in place (no `.temper/` copy tree) and writes a manifest the gate reads clean.

tests.asserts: `temper init` scans a harness into a manifest + skeleton over members in place — zero file moves, byte-identical members — and `check` reads that manifest green; in-place members live-extract and cannot drift.

acceptance: `temper init [<harness-path>]` generates the config skeleton over members in place (no copy tree); `init --lift` migrates one member up a rung; check live-extracts in-place members; emit skips them; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The on-ramp flip (Decision: adoption is a gradient — rungs gate-only / init / altitude, one manifest shape). Rejects migration-into-a-mirror (the retired import as a toll booth). Discovery already honors ignore rules + excludes .git (WALK-IGNORE-DISCIPLINE shipped) so an any-depth scan won't import a dep tree's members. Altitude lift (module carriage) needs the parked TS SDK — not this entry.

summary: Reconcile `emit` and `install` so a whole-file re-emit no longer drops install's schema modeline / managed-by note — the two-projectors collision

per.section: Decision: surface authority is a declared posture, never a baked stance

edit.description: emit's `project_bytes` (drift.rs:553) currently re-emits the frontmatter block from `fields` only and never reads the on-disk projection, so it DROPS the schema modeline (a YAML comment) + reflows the YAML on every run. Make emit PRESERVE the install-placed lines: the modeline + managed-by note ride install (never emit-stamped — law 5 keeps the projection content-faithful), so a whole-file re-emit must carry those non-authored metadata lines through instead of clobbering them.

Align the modeline/note placement (`project_modeline` install.rs:630, `project_note` install.rs:653) with emit's preservation so the two projectors no longer fight over the same file: install owns placing/auditing them, emit round-trips them. Ends the `gate_installed` re-nudge loop that papers over emit dropping them.

Add a regression: place the modeline via install, then emit — assert the modeline + managed-by note SURVIVE (are not dropped/reflowed). Keep `the_managed_by_note_is_never_written_by_apply` (rename to emit) — the note still rides install, never emit-stamped.

tests.asserts: an install-placed schema modeline + managed-by note survive a subsequent `emit` (preserved, not dropped or reflowed); emit still never stamps them itself.

acceptance: `emit` re-emits a rule/skill projection without dropping install's schema modeline or managed-by note; the install↔emit re-nudge loop is gone; the note is still install-placed, never emit-stamped (law 5); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The 'two projectors, one file' ledger residual, tail of the wave. Per the surface-authority Decision the modeline/note ride install (never emit-stamped — law 5), so emit's whole-file re-emit must PRESERVE those install-placed lines, not own-and-stamp them. Landing this lifts the interim 'do not run bare apply/emit on rules' discipline.

summary: Extend the extraction algebra with the two primitives 15-kinds lists but code lacks — a `fenced` block extractor and nested key-path `field` lookup — held until a consumer kind needs them

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the extraction vocabulary is harvested from the built-in kinds and extended deliberately (15-kinds.md Decision: extraction is a closed algebra); no current kind (skill/rule/memory) reads a fenced block or a nested key-path, so adding either now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it. Revive when a kind demands it — a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field, or a spec/class kind's fenced-block extraction. (NB `at-import` is the DIRECTIVES wave's directive primitive, a separate edge primitive — not one of these.)

edit.description: Two closed-vocabulary additions to `Primitive` (`specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`): (1) a `Fenced` primitive extracting fenced code-block content — the markdown-structure extractor the spec lists (`a fenced block`) but the enum omits (today: field/headings/sections/line_count/placement); (2) make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises — the impl currently does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. Both extend the closed-vocab load-guard's accepted primitive set (parse_primitive). This entry owns both edits so they don't collide in parallel worktrees.

The extractor helpers behind the two primitives: a `body_fenced_blocks`-style reader over the markdown body (mirroring `body_headings`/`body_sections`), and key-path traversal for the field extractor (walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice, `specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`).

tests.asserts: a `fenced` primitive yields the fenced block's content and skips prose; a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; both preserve the source scalar kind.

acceptance: `Primitive::Fenced` and a nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` are in the closed vocabulary, load-guarded, and extract over a `Unit`; a KIND.md declaring either reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes one.)
notes: Disk re-confirmed both gaps: `Primitive` (now incl. `Directives`, shipped) still lacks `Fenced`; `Field` reads flat `frontmatter.get(key)`, no key-path; guard is parse_primitive. Neither breaks anything today (no kind uses them). ONE entry — both touch src/kind.rs+extract.rs. NOT the DIRECTIVES wave's `at-import`. No file overlap with the floor-migration chain (kind.rs/extract.rs vs drift/import/check).

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

per.section: Decision: package binding is by artifact kind

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register its kind->package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero (add "agent" to BUILTIN_KINDS + an AGENT_PACKAGE const/binding in builtin.rs; build.rs auto-embeds). Stays deferred: wrong direction under requirements/satisfies. Shares src/kind.rs with EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS — serialize if both un-deferred.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Only `.github/workflows/temper.yml` (CI) exists — no release.yml yet. Parked until a human sets creds up.
